sglang.title: [AMD][diffusion] Temporal-unfolded batched Conv2D for ROCm VAE decode
sglang.author: yctseng0211
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR adds an optional Conv3D→Conv2D transformation (mathematically equivalent) for the ROCm VAE decode path. The key idea is temporal unfolding: a 3D convolution with kernel (Kt, Kh, Kw) can be decomposed into a batched 2D convolution by unfolding the input along the temporal axis.
Usa

sglang.title: [AMD] Replace naive triton RMSNorm with aiter RMSNorm for diffusion model
sglang.author: yichiche
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Replace the default Triton-based RMSNorm with aiter's optimized HIP RMSNorm kernel in the diffusion multimodal_gen path on ROCm, reducing per-kernel RMSNorm time by ~30% (430 us → 290 us, ~0.2% of denoise total time).
Modifications
Key changes in layernorm.py:

Add forward_aiter method to

Failed - fail the docs build

This PR updates those examples to the recommended vllm serve <model> CLI form while preserving t

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix test_whisper distributed test process handling
vllm.author: dzhengAP
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Follow-up to #41423

Add method parameter to multi_gpu_test — allows callers to explicitly specify spawn/fork instead of always defaulting to platform detection. This avoids the need for a separate @create_new_process_for_each_test("spawn") decorator on top of @multi_gpu_test.
Use method="spawn" for

vllm.title: [CI][Bugfix] Make test_gpt2_cache_hit observable across V1 EngineCore
vllm.author: haosdent
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix tests/compile/test_aot_compile.py::test_gpt2_cache_hit. The test patched make_symbol in the parent process, but in V1 the compile runs inside an EngineCore subprocess so the patch never reached the counter. Related to #41953, which made the spawn wrapper eagerly import the test module an

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Preserve leading/trailing whitespace in GLM non-streaming tool parser
vllm.author: rishaps
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
GLM's non-streaming tool parser currently calls value.strip() on completed <arg_value> text before parsing it, which for string types can remove important leading or trailing whitespace, causing incorrect parsing results in code-editing tools, diffs etc...
For example, when the model generat

vllm.title: Require C++20 for compatibility with PyTorch
vllm.author: r-barnes
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
PyTorch headers now enforce C++20 as a minimum (torch/all.h and ATen/ATen.h emit a #error if __cplusplus < 202002L). Building vLLM against such a PyTorch fails because vLLM's build system was still setting C++17.

CMakeLists.txt: CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17 → 20
cmake/cpu_extension.cmake: same
cma
